idea.hook: What if Earth was suddenly TWICE its size?
facts: Gravity would be 2.8 times stronger, making an average 180-pound person weigh over 500 pounds.
The atmosphere would be 8 times denser, reaching pressures similar to diving over 200 feet deep in the ocean – like submerging the Statue of Liberty.
Our day would shorten! A larger planet spins faster to conserve angular momentum, potentially shrinking a 24-hour day to just 6-8 hours. Imagine that!
idea.payoff: Our world would be unrecognizable, forcing life to adapt or perish. Could humanity survive?
idea.title: Earth Grew DOUBLE?! 🤯 Our Planet's Wild Fate!
idea.description: Imagine Earth suddenly doubled in size! 🤯 What would happen to our gravity, our air, and even the length of our day? Dive into a mind-bending hypothetical that reveals the delicate balance keeping our planet just right. Could life as we know it even exist?

scenes.text: What if Earth was suddenly TWICE its size?
scenes.visual: A vibrant Earth slowly zooms out and a larger, glowing version expands behind it, signifying its new size.

scenes.text: Gravity: 2.8x STRONGER! An 180lb person would weigh over 500 lbs.
scenes.visual: An animated human figure struggles against an unseen force, numbers like '180lb' changing to '504lb' next to them. Earth in the background.

scenes.text: Atmosphere: 8x DENSER! Pressure similar to diving 200ft deep (Statue of Liberty submerged).
scenes.visual: The Statue of Liberty appears, slowly submerging into a dark blue, high-pressure environment, bubbles indicating extreme depth.

scenes.text: Day Shortens! From 24 hours to just 6-8 hours. A larger planet spins FASTER.
scenes.visual: A rapidly spinning Earth with a digital clock in the corner flashing '24:00' then quickly accelerating to '06:00'.

scenes.text: Our world would be unrecognizable. Life adapts or perishes. Could humanity survive?
scenes.visual: A dramatic, unrecognizable Earth landscape. Perhaps stormy skies, colossal mountains, or dense, fog-shrouded terrain. A sense of awe and danger.
